% Auto-generated by scripts/06_emit_structure.py — Phase 3.
% This is a minimal placeholder. Phase 10 (LaTeX assembly) will refine it.
\documentclass[11pt,a4paper]{book}

\usepackage{fontspec}
\usepackage{xunicode}
\usepackage{xeCJK}
% Noto Serif CJK SC ships glyph coverage across Simplified, Traditional, JP, KR
% — single font avoids missing-glyph issues with simplified-only fonts.
\setCJKmainfont{Noto Serif CJK SC}
\usepackage{polyglossia}
\setdefaultlanguage{english}
\usepackage{microtype}
\usepackage[margin=1in]{geometry}
\usepackage[backend=biber,style=authoryear,natbib]{biblatex}
\usepackage{csquotes}
\usepackage{graphicx}
\usepackage{booktabs}
\usepackage{makeidx}
\makeindex
\usepackage{tabularx}
\usepackage{array}
\usepackage{fancybox}

% Phase 3 macros
% \origsecnum{X.Y} - prints original 1978 section number, suppressing LaTeX auto-counter
% Usage: \section[shortname]{\origsecnum{X.Y}Full Name} will print only "X.Y Full Name"
\newcommand{\origsecnum}[1]{%
  \setcounter{secnumdepth}{-1}%    % Suppress section numbering temporarily
  \textup{#1}\quad%                 % Print the original number
  \setcounter{secnumdepth}{2}%     % Re-enable numbering for subsequent sections
}
\newcommand{\authorbyline}[1]{\par\noindent\emph{by #1}\par\bigskip}
\newcommand{\ednote}[1]{\textsuperscript{[#1]}}

% Phase 9: Oracle Inscription Formatting Macros
% ================================================
% Macros recreate the original 1978 book format: two-column layout with
% labels on the left and aligned paired inscriptions (positive/negative)

% Reference/attribution formatter - parenthetical in italics
% Usage: \inscriptionref{拼編 235.2}
\newcommand{\inscriptionref}[1]{%
  \textit{(#1)}%
}

% Inscription component label formatter - bold parenthetical
% Usage: \inscriptionlabel{Preface}
\newcommand{\inscriptionlabel}[1]{%
  \textbf{(#1:)}%
}

% Inscription side label - for "positive, left side" etc.
% Usage: \inscriptionside{positive, left side}
\newcommand{\inscriptionside}[1]{%
  \emph{#1}%
}

% Centered section header for inscription groups
% Usage: \inscriptionsection{PAIR OF COMPLEMENTARY CHARGES}
\newcommand{\inscriptionsection}[1]{%
  \par\vspace{0.5\baselineskip}
  \noindent\hfill\textbf{\MakeUppercase{#1}}\hfill\par
  \noindent\rule{\textwidth}{0.5pt}\par
  \vspace{0.3\baselineskip}
}

% Two-column inscription environment - simplified to prose-based layout
% Wraps inscription content without table structure to avoid LaTeX nesting issues
\newenvironment{inscriptionblock}
{%
  \par\medskip
  \noindent
  \begin{minipage}{\textwidth}
  \setlength{\parskip}{0.3\baselineskip}
  \setlength{\parindent}{0pt}
}
{%
  \end{minipage}
  \medskip
}

% Bibliography (Phase 5)
\addbibresource{bibliography/keightley.bib}

% Numbered list item macro (Phase 10)
% Formats inline numbered items while preserving prose flow
% Marks items structurally for OCR validation and text analysis
% Usage: Among factors are \listitem{1} the density; \listitem{2} the topic; ...
% Renders as: Among factors are (1) the density; (2) the topic; ...
\newcommand{\listitem}[1]{%
  \textnormal{(\textbf{#1})}%
}

% Phase 13 groundwork: chapter-aware pinyin macros
% ===============================================
% Source of truth: data/pinyin_terms.tsv
% Generated registrations: tex/generated/pinyin_terms.tex
%
% Usage:
%   \pinyinterm{term-key}  -> first use in a chapter: 漢字 Pinyin
%                             later uses in that chapter: Pinyin
%   \pinyinfull{term-key}  -> always 漢字 Pinyin
%   \pinyintext{term-key}  -> always Pinyin only
%   \pinyinhanzi{term-key} -> always 漢字 only
%
% Pinyin style is data-driven:
%   - person/place => pinyin_plain
%   - title/day    => pinyin_accented
%   - other terms  => accented if present, otherwise plain
\ExplSyntaxOn
\prop_new:N \g_keightley_pinyin_category_prop
\prop_new:N \g_keightley_pinyin_hanzi_prop
\prop_new:N \g_keightley_pinyin_plain_prop
\prop_new:N \g_keightley_pinyin_accented_prop
\prop_new:N \g_keightley_pinyin_seen_prop

\msg_new:nnn { keightley } { unknown-pinyin-key }
  { Unknown~pinyin~key~'#1'.~Add~it~to~data/pinyin_terms.tsv~and~rebuild. }

\cs_new_protected:Npn \keightley_pinyin_register:nnnnn #1#2#3#4#5
  {
    \prop_gput:Nnn \g_keightley_pinyin_category_prop {#1} {#2}
    \prop_gput:Nnn \g_keightley_pinyin_hanzi_prop {#1} {#3}
    \prop_gput:Nnn \g_keightley_pinyin_plain_prop {#1} {#4}
    \prop_gput:Nnn \g_keightley_pinyin_accented_prop {#1} {#5}
  }

\cs_new_protected:Npn \keightley_pinyin_assert_known:n #1
  {
    \prop_if_in:NnF \g_keightley_pinyin_hanzi_prop {#1}
      { \msg_error:nnn { keightley } { unknown-pinyin-key } {#1} }
  }

\cs_new:Npn \keightley_pinyin_hanzi_value:n #1
  {
    \keightley_pinyin_assert_known:n {#1}
    \prop_item:Nn \g_keightley_pinyin_hanzi_prop {#1}
  }

\cs_new:Npn \keightley_pinyin_plain_value:n #1
  {
    \keightley_pinyin_assert_known:n {#1}
    \prop_item:Nn \g_keightley_pinyin_plain_prop {#1}
  }

\cs_new:Npn \keightley_pinyin_accented_value:n #1
  {
    \keightley_pinyin_assert_known:n {#1}
    \prop_item:Nn \g_keightley_pinyin_accented_prop {#1}
  }

\cs_new:Npn \keightley_pinyin_text_raw_value:n #1
  {
    \keightley_pinyin_assert_known:n {#1}
    \str_case:enF { \prop_item:Nn \g_keightley_pinyin_category_prop {#1} }
      {
        {person}{\keightley_pinyin_plain_value:n {#1}}
        {place}{\keightley_pinyin_plain_value:n {#1}}
        {title}{\keightley_pinyin_accented_value:n {#1}}
        {day}{\keightley_pinyin_accented_value:n {#1}}
      }
      {
        \tl_if_blank:eTF { \prop_item:Nn \g_keightley_pinyin_accented_prop {#1} }
          { \keightley_pinyin_plain_value:n {#1} }
          { \keightley_pinyin_accented_value:n {#1} }
      }
  }

\cs_new:Npn \keightley_pinyin_text_value:n #1
  {
    \keightley_pinyin_assert_known:n {#1}
    \str_case:enF { \prop_item:Nn \g_keightley_pinyin_category_prop {#1} }
      {
        {title}{\textit{\keightley_pinyin_text_raw_value:n {#1}}}
      }
      { \keightley_pinyin_text_raw_value:n {#1} }
  }

\cs_new:Npn \keightley_pinyin_full_value:n #1
  { \keightley_pinyin_hanzi_value:n {#1}~\keightley_pinyin_text_value:n {#1} }

\cs_new:Npn \keightley_pinyin_possessive_text_value:n #1
  { \keightley_pinyin_text_value:n {#1}'s }

\cs_new:Npn \keightley_pinyin_possessive_full_value:n #1
  { \keightley_pinyin_hanzi_value:n {#1}~\keightley_pinyin_text_value:n {#1}'s }

\cs_new_protected:Npn \keightley_pinyin_use:n #1
  {
    \keightley_pinyin_assert_known:n {#1}
    \prop_if_in:NnTF \g_keightley_pinyin_seen_prop {#1}
      { \keightley_pinyin_text_value:n {#1} }
      {
        \prop_gput:Nnn \g_keightley_pinyin_seen_prop {#1} { seen }
        \keightley_pinyin_full_value:n {#1}
      }
  }

\cs_new_protected:Npn \keightley_pinyin_possessive_use:n #1
  {
    \keightley_pinyin_assert_known:n {#1}
    \prop_if_in:NnTF \g_keightley_pinyin_seen_prop {#1}
      { \keightley_pinyin_possessive_text_value:n {#1} }
      {
        \prop_gput:Nnn \g_keightley_pinyin_seen_prop {#1} { seen }
        \keightley_pinyin_possessive_full_value:n {#1}
      }
  }

\AddToHook{cmd/chapter/before}{\prop_gclear:N \g_keightley_pinyin_seen_prop}

\DeclareRobustCommand{\RegisterPinyinTerm}[5]
  { \keightley_pinyin_register:nnnnn {#1}{#2}{#3}{#4}{#5} }
\DeclareRobustCommand{\pinyinterm}[1]{ \keightley_pinyin_use:n {#1} }
\DeclareRobustCommand{\pinyinfull}[1]{ \keightley_pinyin_full_value:n {#1} }
\DeclareRobustCommand{\pinyintext}[1]{ \keightley_pinyin_text_value:n {#1} }
\DeclareRobustCommand{\pinyinposs}[1]{ \keightley_pinyin_possessive_use:n {#1} }
\DeclareRobustCommand{\pinyinhanzi}[1]{ \keightley_pinyin_hanzi_value:n {#1} }
\ExplSyntaxOff

\input{./generated/pinyin_terms}
